tasks.title: Implement in-memory caching for countries and cities using Caffeine
tasks.description: Add caching layer for frequently requested read-only data like countries and cities to reduce database load.
tasks.details: Add Caffeine dependency version 3.1.8. Create CacheConfig.kt to configure caches with maximumSize and expireAfterWrite TTL. Inject caches into CountryService and CityService. Use cache.get(key) with loader lambda for reads. Invalidate cache on writes.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for cache hit/miss scenarios. Integration tests to verify cache invalidation after create/update operations.

tasks.title: Standardize error messages and implement i18n-ready error constants
tasks.description: Centralize all error messages in a resource file or constants object and standardize language to English for API consistency and future i18n support.
tasks.details:
Create ErrorMessages object with formatted string constants for common errors. Replace inline error strings in exceptions and logs with references to ErrorMessages. Consider integration with i18n libraries for future multi-language support.

Task deferred for later implementation. Per PROGRESS_TRACKING.md, error messages will remain in Russian. This task has low priority and will be completed in a future sprint.
</info added on 2025-11-30T10:29:34.826Z>

Recent validation logic has been added to DatabaseConfig.kt in the init block, covering required fields (host, name, user, password), port range (1-65535), positive values for maxPoolSize, connectionTimeout, idleTimeout, maxLifetime, and minIdle constraints (>=0 and <= maxPoolSize, idleTimeout <= maxLifetime). DatabaseConfigTest.kt includes tests for all boundary cases. Error messages from this validation should also be centralized and standardized as part of this task, ensuring consistency with future API error handling standards (e.g., RFC 7807 or RFC 9457 for structured error responses).
tasks.testStrategy: Verify all error messages are replaced. Test error responses for correct message formatting. Ensure validation error messages from DatabaseConfig.kt are included and follow the standardized format.

tasks.title: Optimize API Usage with Batched Write Operations
tasks.description: Reduce the number of API calls by batching updates and write operations to Task Master AI.
tasks.details: Implement batching logic for write operations such as `update_subtask`, `add_task`, and `expand_task`. Accumulate multiple updates in memory and send them in a single API call to minimize overhead and resource consumption. Ensure batching respects session context and does not delay critical updates excessively.
tasks.testStrategy: Measure API call frequency before and after batching implementation. Confirm that batched calls correctly update Task Master state without data loss. Test system responsiveness and consistency under various workloads.

tasks.title: Integrate Economical AI Models for Main and Fallback Operations
tasks.description: Configure and integrate cost-effective AI models (sonar for main/research, gemini-2.0-flash for fallback) to optimize resource usage.
tasks.details: Set up model selection logic to use the sonar model for main and research tasks and gemini-2.0-flash as a fallback model. Ensure seamless switching between models based on availability and task type. Update configuration templates to reflect these model preferences.
tasks.testStrategy: Test model selection under normal and fallback conditions. Validate that tasks use the correct models as per configuration. Monitor resource usage and cost metrics to confirm optimization.

tasks.title: Implement Filtering and Status Checking for Local Task Operations
tasks.description: Enable efficient local filtering of tasks by status, priority, and dependencies, and implement subtask status checks without API calls.
tasks.details: Develop functions to filter tasks read from `tasks.json` based on status, priority, and dependencies. Implement logic to check and update subtask statuses locally by parsing the `details` field. Ensure these operations are performant and accurate to support local-first management and recovery.
tasks.testStrategy: Test filtering accuracy with various task datasets. Validate subtask status detection and updates locally. Confirm no API calls occur during these operations. Benchmark performance for large task sets.

tasks.title: Add Git Commit Rule After Task Completion
tasks.description: Implement a workflow rule that triggers after a task status is set to 'done' to initiate a git commit.
tasks.details:
Modify `.cursor/rules/TASKMASTER_AUTO_WORKFLOW.mdc` to add a new step after `set_task_status`. This step should check if the task status is 'done' and, if so, proceed to execute the git commit logic. Ensure the rule is non-blocking and gracefully handles errors. The implementation has been completed as follows:
- Step 5 added in TASKMASTER_AUTO_WORKFLOW.mdc for automatic git commit after set_task_status
- New file .cursor/rules/taskmaster/git_commit.mdc created with detailed rules
- Section "Git Commit After Task Completion" added in workflow
- Rule is non-blocking - workflow continues even if git is unavailable
- Commit message template is described in the rules
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test the workflow rule by simulating task completion and verifying that the commit step is triggered only for 'done' tasks. Confirm that workflow continues even if git is unavailable. Test should also validate that the commit message template is correctly applied and that the workflow behaves as expected in edge cases (e.g., no git repo, no changes to commit).

tasks.title: Collect Changed Files and Task Data
tasks.description: Gather detailed information about changed files and task details for generating the commit message, using a structured and documented approach.
tasks.details:
Implement a function or script that performs the following five-step process:
1. Verify if the current directory is a git repository using appropriate git commands.
2. Collect the list of changed files using `git status --porcelain` with detailed parsing to identify file states.
3. Retrieve task data (ID, title, description) locally from `tasks.json` without relying on external APIs.
4. Implement a function `generateCommitMessage` that accepts parameters for changed files and task data to produce a structured commit message.
5. Document all steps, commands, and function usage comprehensively in `git_commit.mdc` for maintainability and clarity.

Include example git commands for repository verification and file collection as part of the implementation documentation.
<info added on 2025-11-30T17:18:42.913Z>
Работа над задачей 37 завершена. В документацию `git_commit.mdc` добавлена детальная информация по всем пяти шагам процесса генерации commit message:

1. **Проверка Git репозитория**:  
   - Примеры команд для проверки наличия git репозитория:  
     - `git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree`  
     - `git status`  
   - Альтернативные методы проверки:  
     - Проверка наличия директории `.git`  
     - Использование `git rev-parse --git-dir`  
   - Пример реализации на Kotlin с обработкой ошибок:  
     ```kotlin
     fun isGitRepository(): Boolean {
         return try {
             val process = Runtime.getRuntime().exec("git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree")
             process.waitFor() == 0
         } catch (e: Exception) {
             false
         }
     }
     ```

2. **Парсинг git status --porcelain**:  
   - Описание формата вывода команды `git status --porcelain`  
   - Статусные коды:  
     - `M` — модифицированный файл  
     - `A` — добавленный файл  
     - `D` — удалённый файл  
     - `R` — переименованный файл (формат: `R oldfile -> newfile`)  
     - `C` — скопированный файл  
     - `??` — неотслеживаемый файл  
   - Реализация функции `parseGitStatusPorcelain` на Kotlin с обработкой всех статусов, включая переименованные и неотслеживаемые файлы  
   - Примеры bash-скриптов для парсинга вывода:  
     ```bash
     git status --porcelain | while read line; do
         status=$(echo "$line" | cut -c1-2)
         file=$(echo "$line" | cut -c4-)
         echo "Status: $status, File: $file"
     done
     ```
   - Примеры вывода и ожидаемых результатов для различных сценариев

3. **Чтение данных задачи из tasks.json**:  
   - Реализация функции `readLocalTask` на Kotlin с поддержкой тегов  
   - Альтернативный метод прямого парсинга JSON с использованием библиотеки Jackson  
   - Примеры использования для извлечения id, title, description:  
     ```kotlin
     data class Task(val id: Int, val title: String, val description: String)
     fun readLocalTask(): Task {
         val json = File("tasks.json").readText()
         return jacksonObjectMapper().readValue(json, Task::class.java)
     }
     ```

4. **Дополнительные разделы**:  
   - **Command Reference**: Справочник всех команд workflow с примерами использования  
   - **Testing the Implementation**: Примеры тестирования парсинга и проверки git репозитория, включая сценарии с различными статусами файлов и ошибками

Вся документация содержит детальные примеры команд, реализации и тестирования для всех пяти шагов процесса генерации commit message.
</info added on 2025-11-30T17:18:42.913Z>

tasks.title: Add Edge Case Tests for Validation, Pagination, and Coordinates
tasks.description: Implement comprehensive unit tests targeting boundary conditions for validation logic, pagination parameters, and coordinate inputs to ensure robust handling of edge cases.
tasks.details: Create two new Kotlin test classes: EdgeCaseValidationTest.kt and PaginationEdgeCaseTest.kt. In EdgeCaseValidationTest.kt, develop tests covering boundary values for coordinate inputs, including NaN, Infinity, and out-of-range values, as well as string inputs with maximum length, minimum length, empty strings, and strings containing only whitespace. In PaginationEdgeCaseTest.kt, implement tests for pagination parameters with edge cases such as negative numbers, extremely large values, and invalid string inputs. Use parameterized tests where appropriate to efficiently cover multiple edge scenarios. Ensure tests use the updated validation API and follow existing project conventions for assertions and test structure. Maintain test isolation and clarity with descriptive test names. Integrate these tests into the existing test suite to run automatically during CI builds.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that all new tests in EdgeCaseValidationTest.kt and PaginationEdgeCaseTest.kt execute successfully without failures. Confirm that tests correctly detect invalid inputs by asserting expected validation errors or exceptions. Run the full test suite to ensure no regressions occur. Use parameterized test reports to confirm coverage of all specified edge cases. Review test logs to ensure descriptive output for each edge case scenario. Additionally, perform code review to ensure tests adhere to project standards and effectively cover boundary conditions as specified.

tasks.title: Conduct Test Quality and Maintainability Assessment
tasks.description: Evaluate test code for readability, duplication, complexity, independence, and test data management using static analysis and manual review.
tasks.details: Use IDE built-in static analysis tools to detect test code smells, duplicated logic, complex methods, and missing assertions. Manually review test helper classes, mock setups, and test data builders for clarity and reusability. Assess test isolation and data management practices.
tasks.testStrategy: Run static analysis tools and verify detection of quality issues. Perform manual code reviews and document maintainability concerns. Validate findings with sample test cases and helper classes.

tasks.title: Analyze Test Execution Performance and Identify Bottlenecks
tasks.description: Measure individual test execution times, identify slow tests and suites, and detect inefficiencies such as database query overhead and setup/teardown delays.
tasks.details: Instrument tests to record execution times per test and per suite. Analyze test logs and metrics to find slow-running tests (>1 second). Investigate integration tests for database query inefficiencies and excessive setup/teardown overhead. Identify candidates for parallel execution.
tasks.testStrategy: Collect and analyze timing data from test runs. Confirm identification of slow tests and bottlenecks. Validate findings by profiling database queries and setup/teardown operations.

tasks.title: Assess Test Stability and Flakiness
tasks.description: Detect flaky tests, timing dependencies, shared state issues, and improper cleanup that cause intermittent test failures.
tasks.details: Analyze test failure patterns and logs to identify non-deterministic behavior. Review tests for timing dependencies and shared mutable state. Check test cleanup procedures to ensure proper isolation. Document tests prone to intermittent failures.
tasks.testStrategy: Monitor test runs over multiple executions to detect flakiness. Validate isolation by running tests in random order and parallel. Confirm cleanup effectiveness by checking for residual state.

tasks.title: Identify Missing Test Scenarios and Edge Cases
tasks.description: Review coverage and test suite to find missing tests for error handling, edge cases, integration scenarios, validation, and performance.
tasks.details: Analyze coverage gaps and business-critical paths to infer missing test scenarios. Cross-check with requirements and known risk areas. Document missing tests for error handling paths, boundary conditions, integration points, and performance-critical code.
tasks.testStrategy: Compare documented missing scenarios against coverage reports and requirements. Validate completeness by consulting with stakeholders and reviewing defect history.

tasks.title: Review Mock Usage and Test Data Patterns
tasks.description: Evaluate mock usage for over-mocking, verification completeness, and setup complexity. Assess test data creation patterns for hardcoding and isolation issues.
tasks.details: Analyze test code to identify mock usage patterns and complexity. Check for missing mock verifications and excessive mocking. Review test data builders and hardcoded data usage. Assess whether test data is isolated and reusable.
tasks.testStrategy: Perform static and manual analysis of mock and test data code. Validate findings by running tests with altered mocks and data to check impact on stability and maintainability.

tasks.title: Compile and Prioritize Improvement Recommendations
tasks.description: Consolidate findings from coverage, quality, performance, stability, and gap analyses into a prioritized list of actionable improvements with effort and impact estimates.
tasks.details: Aggregate all identified issues and missing scenarios. Prioritize based on criticality, impact, and effort required. Prepare actionable recommendations with clear descriptions, estimated effort, and expected benefits. Format recommendations for stakeholder review.
tasks.testStrategy: Review recommendations for completeness and clarity. Validate prioritization criteria with stakeholders. Ensure recommendations are feasible and aligned with project constraints.
